claim: Verifier confirms: gathered evidence from explorer and cross-checker is sufficient to support PRD readiness; all evidence has project scope with concrete paths, no assumptions are promoted to facts, and unresolved blockers are correctly reflected in PRD readiness state.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots implement SHA256-based boundary audit
evidence.why: Explorer and cross-checker both cite this implementation as core evidence for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshots captured before and after agent execution; mutations trigger blockers and prd_ready=false
evidence.why: Both agents cite runtime integration proving boundary audit is enforced.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness requires complete evidence results for all roles under cross_check policy
evidence.why: Evidence that missing evidence results create blockers preventing PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines three-agent protocol with writes_code=false
evidence.why: Protocol contract establishing read-only constraint cited by both agents.

evidence.summary: Cross-check PRD readiness test validates EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blockers for missing agents
evidence.why: Test evidence proving all three evidence agents must complete before PRD readiness passes.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates blockers when evidence lacks project scope or paths
evidence.why: Scope validation logic that prevents external URLs from satisfying project fact requirements.

evidence.summary: External-only evidence test validates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker
evidence.why: Test proving scope validation enforces project-scoped evidence for existing-project facts.

evidence.summary: Project evidence path locator test validates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: Test proving concrete file locators are required for project-scoped evidence.
Dispatch plan declares boundary constraints before agent execution.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt includes boundary instructions and agent_tool_profile
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary constraints are communicated directly to agents.
Evidence agent execution requires explicit authorization flags.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines required fields for evidence results
evidence.why: Schema contract enforcing structured evidence cited by both agents.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult sets status=blocked and recommendation=block for malformed or failed results
evidence.why: Evidence that malformed agent results are converted to blocked status.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement detects conflicts and adds EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT blockers
evidence.why: Evidence that cross-checker disagreement cannot be averaged away.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns [explorer, cross-checker, verifier] for cross_check policy
evidence.why: Evidence that three-agent protocol is required for careful routing.

risks: If boundary snapshot fails to detect mutations due to filesystem race conditions or ignored files, PRD readiness could pass despite project changes.
If evidence agents circumvent boundary instructions through external tooling or indirect writes, the harness may not block corrupted demands.
If provider-adapter shell execution escapes boundary snapshot visibility, mutations could occur undetected.

result.notes: Verifier confirms: (1) No assumptions are presented as facts; both explorer and cross-checker have empty assumptions arrays. (2) All evidence records have project scope with concrete repo-relative paths and line numbers. (3) Current blockers in demand state correctly reflect missing evidence agent results, which will be resolved by this verifier completion. (4) Evidence agreement passes with no conflicts between explorer and cross-checker recommendations. (5) PRD readiness should pass once this verifier result is processed, provided no boundary mutations occurred during evidence gathering.
